pulls.title: PRISM Updates: Phased PQC Transition & Cryptographic Agility
pulls.author: satokan
pulls.body:
# PR Description: Implement Phase 1-3 PQC Transition and PRISM Updates

This PR incorporates Satoru Kanno as co-author and comprehensively addresses requirements for Cryptographic Agility and Protocol Security. It defines the phased Post-Quantum Cryptographic transition aligning with CNSA 2.0 timelines and IETF guidance.

## Key Changes

The previous monolithic commit has been successfully split into 4 logical atomic commits to ease the review process:

1. **Authorship & Abstract Alignment**
   - Added Satoru Kanno as co-author
   - Kept the draft name suffix as `-latest` for automated builds.
   - Updated the abstract and design goals section to highlight the phased PQC transition readiness.
2. **Control Protocol Fixes (Section 9)**
   - Fixed the exact Message Header calculation to 76 octets.
   - Fixed the HMAC-SHA-384 ASCII block representation (allocated exactly 12 32-bit rows for 384 bits).
   - Sorted the Message Type identifiers logically (`0x0F` ERROR placed correctly before `0x10`).
3. **Reference Updates & ECH Fixes**
   - `RFC 2119` has been moved to Normative References.
   - Added Post-Quantum and Encryption draft specifications to Informative References (e.g., `draft-ietf-pquip-pqc-engineers-06`, `draft-ietf-tls-hybrid-design-12`, `draft-ietf-tls-mlkem-07`, `draft-ietf-tls-esni-25`).
   - Fixed the first author initial misattribute of `RFC 9370` (Multiple Key Exchanges for IKEv2) from V. to CJ.
4. **PQC Phased Transition & ECH Logic Update (Sections 4 & 11)**
   - Replaced old ECH mention (`RFC 9460`) with the actual protocol specification draft `I-D.ietf-tls-esni`.
   - Replaced Section 11 entirely to map directly to CNSA 2.0 algorithm sets across 3 defined operational phases (Phase 1: ECC/AES, Phase 2: Hybrid, Phase 3: Pure ML-KEM/ML-DSA).
   - Corrected Phase 3 deadlines mapping exact equipment classes (Dec 2030, 2033 hardware timelines) rather than uniform January dates.
   - Refined grammar in Section 4.2 (`create unique fingerprints`) and added the `CONDUIT` encrypted tunnel transmission mandate in Section 9.1.
